# tfregistry_modules (Data Source)

Lists registry modules in an HCP Terraform / Terraform Enterprise organization.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List all registry modules in an organization.
data "tfregistry_modules" "all" {
  organization = "my-organization"
}

# List only private registry modules in an organization.
data "tfregistry_modules" "private" {
  organization  = "my-organization"
  registry_name = "private"
}

# Search for modules by name.
data "tfregistry_modules" "vpc" {
  organization = "my-organization"
  search       = "vpc"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `organization` (String) Name of the organization. If omitted, organization must be defined in the provider config.
- `registry_name` (String) Whether to list modules from the public or private registry. Must be either "public" or "private".
- `search` (String) A search query to filter modules by name.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Synthetic identifier for the data source result.
- `modules` (Attributes List) The list of registry modules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--modules))

<a id="nestedatt--modules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `modules`

Read-Only:

- `created_at` (String) Time when the module was created.
- `id` (String) The ID of the registry module.
- `name` (String) The name of the module.
- `namespace` (String) The namespace of the module.
- `organization` (String) The organization the module belongs to.
- `provider` (String) The provider of the module.
- `registry_name` (String) Whether the module is in the public or private registry.
- `status` (String) The status of the module.
- `updated_at` (String) Time when the module was last updated.
